questions.question: X-rays were discovered by ________
questions.explanation: Wilhelm Roentgen was the first person to discover electromagnetic radiation in a wavelength range commonly known as X-rays.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT made of quarks?
questions.explanation: Protons and neutrons are built out of still more elementary constituents called quarks.

questions.question: Range of which fundamental force is very shorter?
questions.explanation: The range of weak nuclear force is exceedingly small, of the order of 10⁻¹⁶ m.

questions.question: One Angstrom is equal to 0.1 ___________
questions.explanation: One angstrom is a unit of length equal to10⁻¹⁰ m (one ten-billionth of a metre) or 0.1 nanometer. Its symbol is Å.

questions.question: Which among the following changes in an Alternating current?
questions.explanation: Alternating current (AC) is an electric current which periodically reverses direction, in contrast to direct current (DC) which flows only in one direction.

questions.question: Which among the following is the most energetic of all?
questions.explanation: Radio waves have photons with low energies, microwave photons have a little more energy than radio waves, infrared photons have still more, then visible, ultraviolet, X-rays, and, the most energetic of all, gamma-rays.

questions.question: FM radio stations use ________
questions.explanation: FM broadcasting is a method of radio broadcasting using frequency modulation (FM) technology. It uses Very High Frequencies.

questions.question: Radio communication is made possible because of presence of which layer of atmosphere?
questions.explanation: The electrified ions that fill the whole of the ionospheric layer that completely surrounds the Earth reflect radio waves from all directions to every part of the world. It makes radio communications possible.

questions.question: In a cryogenic rocket engine, which among the following process is done?
options: Gases liquefied and stored at very low temperatures
Liquid fuel is converted into gas and stored at room temperature
Liquid fuel is solidified at high temperature and stored
Solid fuel is converted into gaseous fuel and stored
Gaseous fuel is stored at ultra-high temperature
questions.explanation: A cryogenic rocket engine is a rocket engine that uses a cryogenic fuel or oxidizer, that is, its fuel or oxidizer (or both) are gases liquefied and stored at very low temperatures.

questions.question: Mercury is used in thermometers because of which among the following properties?
questions.explanation: Mercury is used in thermometers because it has high coefficient of expansion.

questions.question: Nomadic Elephant is joint exercise between India and __________
questions.explanation: It is a Indo-Mongolia joint training Exercise. The aim of this exercise is to develop synergy and inter operability between the two armies to fight in Counter Insurgency and Counter Terrorism environment under the United Nation mandate.

questions.question: Who is the first women Defence Minister of India?
questions.explanation: First woman to head India' defence ministry was Indira Gandhi: first in 1975 and again in 1980-82.

questions.question: Armed Forces Medical College is located in __________
questions.explanation: The Armed Forces Medical College is a medical college in Pune, India, in the state of Maharashtra. The college is managed by the Indian Armed Forces.

questions.question: Which among the following is an anti-tank missile?
questions.explanation: Nag is a third generation "Fire-and-forget" anti-tank missile developed in India. It is one of five missile systems developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation.

questions.question: Sashastra Seema Bal was founded in ________
questions.explanation: Sashastra Seema Bal or SSB, the Armed Border Force, is one of India's Central Armed Police Forces. It was founded in 1963.

questions.question: Which of the following military operation was launched to control LTTE?
questions.explanation: Operation Viraat (1988) was an anti-insurgency operation launched by the IPKF against the LTTE in April 1988 in Northern Sri Lanka.

questions.question: "Service Before Self" is the motto of __________
questions.explanation: The National Defence Academy is the Joint Services academy of the Indian Armed Forces, where cadets of the three services, the Army, the Navy and the Air Force train together. Its motto is "Service Before Self".

questions.question: Which is associated with Peacetime distinguished service medals?
questions.explanation: Param Vishisht Seva Medal (PVSM) is a military award of India. It was constituted in 1960 and since then till date, it is awarded in recognition to peace-time service of the most exceptional order and may be awarded posthumously.

questions.question: Insignia having Two five-pointed stars are held by which rank of officers in Indian Army?
questions.explanation: Lieutenant Rank is achieved on commissioning into Indian Army as an Officer. It has two- five-pointed stars as insignia.

questions.question: India shares longest border with which country?
questions.explanation: Bangladesh and India share longest border of 4,096 km international border. It is also the fifth-longest land border in the world.

questions.question: Blue baby syndrome is caused by contamination of groundwater by ___________?
questions.explanation: "Blue Baby Syndrome" is caused by nitrate contamination in groundwater resulting in decreased oxygen carrying capacity of haemoglobin in babies leading to death.

questions.question: Yellow revolution is associated with _______
questions.explanation: Yellow revolution refers to increased production of oilseeds.

questions.question: Artificial trans-fat is produced by adding which gas to vegetable oils?
questions.explanation: Artificial trans fats (or trans fatty acids) are created in an industrial process that adds hydrogen to liquid vegetable oils to make them more solid.

questions.question: Aqua regia is a mixture of hydrochloric acid (HCl) and _______
questions.explanation: Aqua regia is a mixture of hydrochloric acid (HCl) and nitric acid (HNO3) at a ratio of either 3:1 or 4:1. Aqua regia to dissolve the noble metals gold, platinum, and palladium.

questions.question: Marsh gas primarily consist of ___________
questions.explanation: It is a gaseous decomposition product of organic matter, consisting primarily of methane.

questions.question: Rickets is caused by deficiency of which vitamin?
questions.explanation: Rickets is a skeletal disorder that's caused by a lack of vitamin D, calcium, or phosphate.

questions.question: Increased secretion of calcium oxalate leads to ________
questions.explanation: Calcium oxalate stones are the most common type of kidney stone. Calcium oxalate stones are caused by too much oxalate in the urine.

questions.question: Itai-itai disease was caused by poising by ________
questions.explanation: Itai-itai disease was caused by cadmium poisoning due to mining. It is a disorder found in Japan.

questions.question: Guard cells in plants lower their water potential by accumulating which ions?
questions.explanation: The guard cells lower their water potential to draw in water from the surrounding epidermal cells, by actively accumulating potassium ions. This requires energy in the form of ATP which, is supplied by the chloroplasts in the guard cells.

questions.question: Myopic condition refers to _______
questions.explanation: Nearsightedness, or myopia, is a vision condition in which people can see close objects clearly, but objects farther away appear blurry.
